questionHtml:
Alcohol levels in the breath can also be determined using IR spectroscopy.

Suggest, giving a reason, which bond’s absorbance is most useful for detecting ethanol in breath.

Bond: 

Reason:
markschemeHtml:
Bond:
C–O
OR
C–H  [✔]

Reason:
cannot use O–H bonds as in water «found in breath» [✔]

 

Note: Accept “C–O/C–H «bonds in molecules in breath» most likely to be in ethanol”.

Do not apply ECF here.

meta.title: Alcohol levels in the breath can also be determined using IR spectroscopy. Suggest, giving a reason, which bond’s absorbance is most useful for detecting ethanol in breath. Bond: R
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 3.2—Functional groups: Classification of organic compounds

Structure 3.2.9—Infrared (IR) spectra can be used to identify the type of bond present in a molecule. Interpret the functional group region of an IR spectrum, using a table of characteristic frequencies (wavenumber/cm–1).
